Fungi Covered Great Staff  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Charges: Unlimited
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 35
DMG: 18 Dmg Bonus: 130
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
CHA: -10 WIS: +8 INT: +8
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5
Effect: Fungal Regrowth (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 35
WT: 1.1 Size: MEDIUM
Class: DRU SHM BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Slot: PRIMARY
Charges: Unlimited

DMG: 18 Dmg Bonus: 130
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5
Effect: Fungal Regrowth (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 35

Class: DRU SHM BST BER
Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Stackable:No

# Feb 07 2005 at 12:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591798315585
51 posts
Fungus Covered one is back....sort of. They have now added it to the casino along with the Holgrash (sp) beads, the DE mask, and other items. I think it's SOE so called answer to the economy problem. Put some really sweet items that they once deemed to powerful in the casino in hopes people with all the money will blow all their pp there and every one will have to lower prices to sell anything cause of all the broke gambling addicts trying to get the sweet items. Heh only works in reverse though...now folks gonna charge more for items to support their gambling habits lol. ;)

FungI is nerfed

Just wanted to add, confirmed this does NOT cancel out the Spiritual Light/Radiance/Purity/Dominion line which has hp and mana regen both. Good news for us beasties.

# Apr 07 2004 at 7:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591746804387
2 posts
Does this effect stack with the tunic?
▲ Page top
RE: Staff + Tunic = ?
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2004 at 10:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592265132527
38 posts
Yes, it's a buff, while the fungus tunic is a worn effect. Druids and Shamen can do both at once and get 30hp per tick, that's what I do.

staff and sow
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2004 at 5:57 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591795494063
11 posts
Dont know if mentioned before but you can have sow on while this effect is on, and click the effect and start running at sow again, just make sure sow is casted after you click the staff effect.
▲ Page top
RE: staff and sow
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2004 at 8:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760131119
View User Forum
330 posts
Currently, SoW will overwrite fungal regrowth but you can just click fungal regrowth back on to have both effects on. However, you will see no movement increase in having both fungal regrowth AND SoW on over just fungal regrowth. To recieve the movement increases from SoW, you must click off fungal regrowth. It's just that now you don't have to recast SoW every time you want to travel.
▲ Page top

click on, click off...the clicker
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2003 at 3:30 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591768204909
372 posts
Click the staff while holding to cast. Instant cast time. Slows you down to a crawl. Click the spell icon to cancel, lets you resume walking again. Pity this cancels SoW. Have yet to test as to whether or not it cancel's horse summons, or works properly with it or not.
▲ Page top
RE: click on, click off...the clicker
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2003 at 10:53 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592760748740
1 post
My shammy uses this staff. As of the last patch it no longer cancels out SoW. You can click it off any time to resume running at normal speed.

It does not cancel out summoned mounts, horses or lizards either. Althought it will still snare you.

Stabbi
Altruistic Valorians
Morell Thule
▲ Page top
RE: click on, click off...the clicker
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2004 at 8:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760131119
View User Forum
330 posts
I have the pre nerf version and I also have a 100k Drogmor. I don't know if this works different for lower cost ones or horses... But, for me, it has zero effect on my forewards and backwards movement while on my mount. The only thing it effects is my side to side movement.

Also, it doesn't effect your swim speed. Works great in Plane of Water.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Feb 02 2003 at 3:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756276455
20 posts
Just wondering if it is possible to click off this effect or if it is necessary to debuff yourself to end the effect early. Thanks.
▲ Page top
No longer stacks with Regrowth
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2002 at 4:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591779825529
19 posts
Just to clarify the below posts. The click effect (which only works when equipped) on the staff NO LONGER stacks with Regrowth. It doesn't matter where SoW or Regrowth are in the buff order, the effect overwrites both buffs.

This is still one incredible Beastlord staff.

Regards,

Brelk

Edited, Sat Aug 17 17:38:24 2002

# Jan 05 2003 at 2:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966118024
319 posts
this is his rarest drop

Robe = common

Fungi tunic = uncommon

Fungi staff = rare

▲ Page top
Staff
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2002 at 1:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592247986931
56 posts
Fungi Covered Great Staff:
clicks instant fungal regrowth at lvl 35
only works on you, and dont have to have yourself targeted
must be equipped for effect
effect overwrites SoW and other positive (meaning not snare/ensnaring dots etc) movement rate adjusting spells
does not stack with regen, chloro etc
gives about a 10-20 percent slow in walk/run speed ending up being a little faster than regular walk
does NOT slow your attack speed
*mobs will be able to outrun you* but clicks off just fine then you can jump away
regens 15hp/tick like regular regrow
duration is 50 ticks (300 seconds)

Hopefully this coveres everything =)
Very nice staff btw.

Edited, Thu Jun 20 02:28:01 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Staff
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2004 at 8:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760131119
View User Forum
330 posts
It's a 42% snare at level 65, more specificly. 10% snare at level 1. Scales between 10% and 42% based on level.
▲ Page top
stack
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 10:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361900358

117 posts


Edited, Thu Mar 7 22:10:27 2002
▲ Page top
Level it procs at I promise!
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2002 at 2:58 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251070326
187 posts
I got this for my twinked out beastlord, had it since level 30 every level I test it and at level 35 it clicks! THATS RIGHT AT 35!!!!!! not 40, as of today 2/1/02. Also its instant so I can pull send pet and cast regen then switch out weapons to my good damage ones and attack, before pet takes much damage therefor dont need to heal him. Currently I am fighting snow cougars in Iceclad and I have no downtime but to buff myself and pet. Not sure bout shammy or druids but this is GREAT for Beastlord.

Edited, Fri Feb 1 01:58:58 2002
